orank on-site: content efficiency

orank scored the homepage Content efficiency 1/2: "Low content efficiency: 4.63% — most of the page is markup, not content." The threshold is 5%. This adds real descriptive prose to the server-rendered "What is OrchestKit?" section (not filler) to cross it, and names the developer resources by name as an honest side benefit.

Measured, before and after (prerendered SSR HTML)

before   7162 text / 150238 html = 4.77%
after    8320 text / 147957 html = 5.62%

Amber line = 5% threshold. +1158 text chars, all in the raw HTML an AI crawler reads without running JavaScript.

The three "on-site" candidates, and what was honest to touch

orank checkActionWhy
Content efficiency 1/2fixedGenuinely measurable and real. 4.77 to 5.62 pct with margin.
Content without JS 2/3 ("flat heading structure")not touchedThe SSR HTML already has h1 + 11 h2 + 22 h3 with a meaningful H1 ("OrchestKit — Stop explaining your stack"). "Flat" is an opaque scanner heuristic; editing on a guess would be churn.
REST deprecation policy 2/3not touchedAlready documented in full: RFC 8594 Deprecation + Sunset headers described in both /api-policy.md and the OpenAPI info.description. The scanner is not parsing the prose. Nothing is deprecated today, so emitting a live Sunset header would be false.

Standing after the trust-manifest PR (#3078) shipped

Score held at 82/B. The trust manifest was a non-scoring bonus on an already-passing check, so it did not move the number (it is still a real correctness fix: the did:web identity now resolves). The remaining lost points are off-site (developer-resource search, Wikipedia, ChatGPT store) or would require fabricating an auth server (auth.md AS chain) — none are honest codebase changes. This content-efficiency fix is the one clean on-site point left.
